title: Science + AI Technology: Kerala Class 9 — Gravitation and Motion
subject: Science

questions.text: ISRO's Chandrayaan-3 orbited the Moon at altitude 100 km. Moon's surface gravity = 1.62 m/s². Approximately what orbital speed was needed? (Hint: use orbital mechanics concept that centripetal acceleration = gravity)
questions.explanation: At orbit altitude h, centripetal acceleration = g_surface × (R/(R+h))². Moon R=1737 km. At 100km: g_orbit ≈ 1.62 × (1737/1837)² ≈ 1.45 m/s². v = √(g_orbit × (R+h)) = √(1.45 × 1837000) ≈ 1633 m/s. Your gravitation chapter guided Chandrayaan-3 to the Moon's south pole — a world first!
questions.subject: Science
